headline_summary: IEA chief says Middle East oil supply disruptions will rise in April as Strait of Hormuz closure bites; diesel and jet fuel shortages flagged (Reuters, Apr 1)

Bahrain circulates revised UN Security Council draft on protecting shipping in/around Strait of Hormuz; keeps 'all necessary means' language but drops explicit Chapter VII enforcement reference (Reuters, Apr 1)
Iran tells UN/IMO that 'non-hostile' vessels may transit Hormuz if coordinated with Iranian authorities, while US/Israel-linked vessels do not qualify (Reuters, Mar 24)
Explosions heard in Damascus attributed by Syrian state TV to Israel intercepting Iranian missiles (Reuters, Mar 31)
Syrian army says drone attacks targeted bases near Iraqi border; says it is assessing response options (Reuters, Mar 30)
Haifa oil refinery site hit by debris from intercepted missile; fire contained and fuel supply not affected, Israel says (Reuters, Mar 30)
Israel orders expanded operations in southern Lebanon to push Hezbollah rocket/ATGM threat away from border (Reuters, Mar 29)
Lebanon prepares for potential long-term displacement as Israeli control plans extend to Litani river, minister says (Reuters, Mar 31)
Houthis signal readiness to reopen Red Sea/Bab al-Mandab shipping front if Iran needs support; Bab al-Mandab highlighted as chokepoint if Hormuz shut (Reuters, Mar 26)
Houthis enter Iran war with attacks on Israel as additional US forces arrive, raising multi-front escalation and shipping risk (Reuters, Mar 28)
risk_drivers: Strait of Hormuz traffic remains near-halted with contested rules for 'safe passage'
UN-level debate over maritime protection underscores elevated risk of direct confrontation at sea
Spillover into Levant (Israel-Lebanon) and Syria air-defense activity increases regional escalation pathways
Potential second chokepoint risk at Bab al-Mandab if Houthis re-open Red Sea attacks
Refined products shortages (diesel/jet) and European vulnerability as disruptions rise in April
Persistent drone/missile incidents increase tail-risk for energy and logistics infrastructure

Energy: Upside skew to crude and refined product cracks as April disruption projections increase and diesel/jet scarcity emerges (Reuters, Apr 1).
Higher probability of a two-chokepoint shock (Hormuz + Bab al-Mandab) if Houthis re-enter maritime campaign, raising delivered energy costs.
FX: Oil-importer EM FX remains vulnerable to higher energy import bills and risk-off sentiment; USD support likely in acute escalation.
Rates: Energy-driven inflation risk keeps breakevens supported and can delay easing expectations; wider credit spreads for energy-intensive and transport sectors.
Equities: Risk-off flows favor energy/defense while airlines, shipping, and industrials with Middle East supply-chain exposure face margin pressure.

sources.title: Middle East oil supply disruptions to rise in April and hit Europe, IEA chief says
Bahrain circulates revised UN Hormuz draft, drops binding enforcement
Iran tells UN: 'non-hostile' ships can transit Strait of Hormuz
Explosions heard in Syria's Damascus result of Israel intercepting Iranian missiles, state TV says
Syrian army says drone attacks targeted its bases near Iraq
Missile hits fuel tanker at Israel's Oil Refineries
Israel's Netanyahu orders expansion of southern Lebanon operations to halt Hezbollah rockets
Lebanon preparing for displacement crisis amid funding crunch, minister says
Yemen's Houthis ready to join Iran war if needed, raising new shipping risk
Yemen's Houthis enter Iran war with attack on Israel, while US Marines arrive in region

events.title: IEA warns Middle East oil disruptions will rise in April as Hormuz closure hits Europe
events.implication: Signals worsening physical disruption into April and highlights refined product (diesel/jet) scarcity risk; supports elevated energy risk premia.

events.title: Bahrain revises UN draft on protecting shipping around Hormuz, dropping explicit Chapter VII enforcement
events.location_name: United Nations Headquarters, New York, United States
events.implication: Shows Gulf/US push for maritime protection while accommodating likely veto constraints; increases focus on convoy/escort options.

events.title: Iran tells UN/IMO that 'non-hostile' vessels can transit Hormuz if coordinated with Iranian authorities
events.implication: Codifies Iran-controlled 'safe passage' framing that can fragment shipping access and keep throughput constrained.

events.title: Explosions in Damascus attributed to Israel intercepting Iranian missiles
events.implication: Highlights aerial spillover into Syrian airspace and risks of miscalculation around air defenses and flight corridors.

events.title: Syrian army reports drone attacks on bases near Iraqi border
events.implication: Suggests cross-border drone threat vector and raises regional escalation risk via attribution disputes (Iraq/Syria theater).

events.title: Haifa oil refinery area hit by debris from intercepted missile; fire contained
events.implication: Demonstrates infrastructure vulnerability and operational risk premium for Israeli energy assets despite limited immediate supply impact.

events.title: Israel orders further expansion of southern Lebanon operations to halt Hezbollah rockets
events.implication: Increases likelihood of sustained cross-border exchanges and displacement; adds to regional risk-off backdrop.

events.title: Lebanon prepares for possible long-term displacement amid Israeli plans to control territory up to Litani
events.implication: Humanitarian and political stress can prolong conflict dynamics and complicate de-escalation.

events.title: Houthis signal readiness to disrupt Bab al-Mandab shipping in solidarity with Iran
events.implication: Raises probability of a second maritime chokepoint shock, amplifying freight and energy delivery costs.

events.title: Houthis launch attacks on Israel as US forces arrive, raising multi-front escalation risk
events.implication: Demonstrates expansion of active fronts and increases tail-risk for Red Sea shipping and broader regional retaliation cycles.

links.from: Bahrain revises UN draft on protecting shipping around Hormuz, dropping explicit Chapter VII enforcement
links.to: Iran tells UN/IMO that 'non-hostile' vessels can transit Hormuz if coordinated with Iranian authorities
links.why: Both center on contested rules and enforcement mechanisms for navigation in/around the Strait of Hormuz, with Bahrain/UN text reacting to the operational slowdown and security risks described in Iranian communications.

links.from: IEA warns Middle East oil disruptions will rise in April as Hormuz closure hits Europe
links.why: IEA guidance points to higher April supply losses and refined product scarcity, increasing upside tail risk for crude and product prices.

links.from: Houthis signal readiness to disrupt Bab al-Mandab shipping in solidarity with Iran
links.why: Potential Bab al-Mandab disruption would compound Hormuz constraints, tightening seaborne supply routes and raising risk premium.

links.from: Houthis launch attacks on Israel as US forces arrive, raising multi-front escalation risk
links.why: Additional front activation and US force movement increase geopolitical uncertainty, typically pushing investors toward safe havens.

links.from: Haifa oil refinery area hit by debris from intercepted missile; fire contained
links.why: Even contained incidents at energy facilities can raise perceived vulnerability and risk premium in regional energy assets.

links.from: IEA warns Middle East oil disruptions will rise in April as Hormuz closure hits Europe
links.why: Higher energy import costs and volatility typically pressure oil-importer EM currencies and increase hedging demand.

links.from: Israel orders further expansion of southern Lebanon operations to halt Hezbollah rockets
links.why: Sustained Israel-Hezbollah combat and displacement risk prolong regional conflict uncertainty.

links.from: IEA warns Middle East oil disruptions will rise in April as Hormuz closure hits Europe
links.why: Energy-driven inflation risk and transport cost shock can widen sector credit spreads and keep rates pricing more hawkish.
